retrieved_docs.title: Hydroxyapatite Dental Material -- Definition/Introduction
retrieved_docs.content: Overall, the focus in hard tissue repair is on hydroxyapatite due to its significant chemical composition, which constitutes the majority of challenging tissue, and its mechanical properties that support tissue integrity. [8] Hydroxyapatite is widely utilized as an implant material because of its excellent osteoconductive properties that facilitate osseointegration and osteogenesis. The raw materials and synthesis processes of hydroxyapatite influence the biological response to its implants, resulting in variations in product properties.

retrieved_docs.title: Silicosis -- Introduction
retrieved_docs.content: Silica also exists as "silica nanoparticles" (SiNPs), particles of nanoform silicon dioxide that measure less than 100 nm in diameter. SiNPs have physiochemical characteristics distinct from the bulk form of silica. [6] SiNPs are colloidal metal oxides comprising a siloxane (Si-O-Si-O) core with abundant silanol (Si-OH) surface groups. SiNPs are either mesoporous or solid. The silica matrix of mesoporous SiNPs has many channels and pores, whereas solid SiNPs have no pores. [7] Mesoporous SiNPs have a controllable, uniform pore size, large volume-to-surface ratio, easily modifiable surface chemistry, and exceptional biocompatibility. [8] The silica framework of SiNPs yields many advantageous qualities, such as its stability in acidic conditions, temperature fluctuations, and organic solvents. [8] The pores of mesoporous SiNPs can be filled with various biomolecules. [7]

retrieved_docs.title: Zirconia Biomaterials -- Issues of Concern
retrieved_docs.content: Heating zirconia at a low temperature (200 to 300 C) can result in the progressive spontaneous transformation of the metastable tetragonal phase into the monoclinic phase in the presence of water, a phenomenon known as low-temperature degradation (LTD). It is a slow transformation that starts in isolated grains on the zirconia surface, leading to an increase in volume. This stresses the adjacent grains and a microcrack appears, allowing water to penetrate and the process to ultimately progress and resulting in a remarkable decrease in strength. This strength degradation is different for various zirconia ceramics, and the variation is related to factors such as stabilizer concentration and distribution, grain size, and the presence of residual stresses. A recent study showed that ceria-stabilized zirconia (12Ce-TZP) was resistant to simulated hydrothermal aging; its flexural strength remained unaffected at a low level of 500 MPa Y-TZP and suffered from low-temperature aging degradation (LTAD) caused by phase transformation.

retrieved_docs.title: Endodontic Materials Used To Fill Root Canals -- Properties -- Gutta-Percha
retrieved_docs.content: Gutta-percha is the most commonly used core obturation material in modern endodontics. [25] They are manufactured as cones comprised mostly of zinc oxide and gutta-percha with added plasticizers and radiopacifiers. [5] Gutta-percha is a naturally occurring thermoplastic material that originates from specific trees in the Malaysian region. [25] The popularity of gutta-percha as an obturation material stems from its variable chemical composition. Gutta-percha exists in 2 crystalline phases, alpha and beta, and can cycle between these phases when heated is applied or removed. The alpha phase of gutta-percha occurs when heated, producing a tacky material that can flow under pressure. Alternatively, the beta phase is when the gutta-percha is unheated, forming a solid mass of compactable material. [1] [25]

retrieved_docs.title: Hydroxyapatite Dental Material -- Issues of Concern
retrieved_docs.content: The mechanical properties of hydroxyapatite are influenced by factors such as phase composition, crystal size, and synthesis method. Pure hydroxyapatite typically has a bending strength of 38 to 250 MPa, compressive strength of 120 to 150 MPa, and tensile strength of 38 to 300 MPa. The Young modulus ranges from 35 to 120 GPa, depending on the presence of impurities. [19] Meanwhile, the Weibull modulus, ranging from 5 to 18, indicates hydroxyapatite is a brittle material. To enhance its mechanical properties, tough hydroxyapatite can be obtained by incorporating TCP, which increases its flexural strength. In contrast, the flexural strength reaches its minimum when hydroxyapatite contains calcium oxide CaO. [10] [19] Furthermore, the sintering temperature also significantly alters the mechanical properties of hydroxyapatite. Higher sintering temperatures lead to increased density, compressive strength, grain size, and torsional strength. [1]

retrieved_docs.title: Hydroxyapatite Dental Material -- Clinical Significance
retrieved_docs.content: Several challenges exist regarding the application of hydroxyapatite in medicine. For instance, using hydroxyapatite as an implant presents inherent defects and fine porosity that can act as crack initiators. Following crack initiation, propagation can lead to catastrophic failure during use. Additionally, the application of bulk hydroxyapatite may result in a modulus mismatch between the bone and the implant, leading to disproportionate load sharing. [10] Conversely, hydroxyapatite, irrespective of its source, always contains trace elements such as fluoride ions (F - ) and hydroxyl ions (OH - ), which contribute to increased crystallite size and decreased solubility, thereby enhancing the strength of the apatite. [19] At the same time, elements such as phosphide ions (PO 3 3- ) and chloride ions (Cl - ) have been shown to weaken the mechanical properties of hydroxyapatite by reducing crystallite size and increasing solubility. [10] [12]

retrieved_docs.title: Zirconia Biomaterials -- Definition/Introduction
retrieved_docs.content: ZrO2 is a white crystalline oxide of zirconium found in the minerals baddeleyite and zircon. Zirconium oxide crystals can exist in three phases: (1) the cubic phase (C) which is a square sided straight prism; (2) the tetragonal phase (T) in the form of a rectangular sided straight prism, and (3) the monoclinic phase (M) with a deformed prism with parallelepiped sides. The cubic phase is stable above 2370 degrees Cand with moderate mechanical properties. The tetragonal phase is stable between 1170°C to 2370 °C with improved mechanical properties. The monoclinic phase is stable at room temperature up to 1170°C, with lower mechanical properties, and may contribute to a reduction of the ceramic particles cohesion.

retrieved_docs.title: Hydroxyapatite Dental Material -- Definition/Introduction
retrieved_docs.content: Hydroxyapatite crystals are naturally present in the human bones and teeth. Hydroxyapatite crystals, as a bioactive ceramic, account for 65% to 70% of its weight in human bone. Furthermore, the architecture of the bone comprises type-I collagen as the organic component and hydroxyapatite as an inorganic component. Together, these 2 components create a nanoscale composite structure, where nano-hydroxyapatite is interspersed within the collagen network. This composite forms mineralized collagen and serves as the precursor for biological mineralized tissues, ranging from tendons and skin to hard tissues such as bone and teeth. Moreover, in bones, hydroxyapatite crystals are plate- or needle-shaped, measuring approximately 40 to 60 nm in length, 20 nm in width, and 1.5 to 5 nm in thickness. [3] The varying sizes and shapes of hydroxyapatite crystals contribute to the structural stability, hardness, and function of these tissues. [4] [5]

retrieved_docs.title: Zirconia Biomaterials -- Review Questions
retrieved_docs.content: Access free multiple choice questions on this topic. Comment on this article.

retrieved_docs.title: Zirconia Biomaterials -- Clinical Significance
retrieved_docs.content: The restorations are processed either by soft machining of pre-sintered blanks or by hard machining of fully sintered blocks. The mechanical properties of 3Y-TZP are strongly affected by its grain size. The mechanical properties of zirconia have been reported to be higher than other ceramics for dental applications. Fracture resistance of 6–10 MPa/m1/2, a flexural strength of 900–1200 MPa, and a compression resistance of 2000 MPa have been reported. Zirconia restorations bear an average load of 755 N. Zirconia yields higher fracture loads than alumina or lithium disilicate. Zirconia ceramics yield superior wear behavior and lower antagonistic wear compared to porcelain.

retrieved_docs.title: Endodontic Materials Used To Fill Root Canals -- Classification -- Root Canal Sealers
retrieved_docs.content: The role of the sealer is critical for establishing a fluid-tight seal of the root canal as it fills the vacant space between the prepared dentinal wall and the core filling material. [1] [5] It also reaches lateral canals and tubules inaccessible to gutta-percha cones and provides antimicrobial action. [5] Most sealers are toxic before setting, so care should be taken to avoid the extrusion of sealers into the periapical tissues. [6] Sealers induce different levels of periapical inflammation; however, this effect is usually transient and does not interfere with tissue healing. [7] Sealers are partly reabsorbed after exposure to tissue fluids. The core material should occupy almost all of the canal, and sealers should only be applied in small quantities. Several types of sealers are available, which vary in formulation and properties. They are classified into 5 main groups according to their composition: zinc oxide eugenol (ZOE) based, calcium hydroxide based, glass ionomer-based, resin-based, and bioceramic sealers. The properties of an ideal sealer have been outlined in the literature. It is important to note that no material has been proven to eliminate leakage nor fulfill all the ideals.

retrieved_docs.title: Zirconia Biomaterials -- Definition/Introduction
retrieved_docs.content: The tetragonal phase can be retained at room temperature by the addition of 3 mol% yttria. Yttria-stabilized tetragonal zirconia polycrystal (Y-TZP) provides mechanical properties superior to other all-ceramic systems due to the tetragonal-to-monoclinic phase transformation. Thus, Y-TZP has been increasingly used as a core material for all-ceramic restorations. [1] [2] [3]

retrieved_docs.title: Black Piedra -- Pathophysiology
retrieved_docs.content: In vivo and in vitro studies using electron microscopy and x-ray microanalysis have revealed the presence of elements such as phosphorus, sulfur, and calcium in the nodules that develop on hair and in culture colonies. These elements are part of the extracellular material that compacts the pseudoparenchymatous organization of the fungus. Their presence is due to the capacity of melanin-like pigments to sequester ions. They may form part of the mucopolysaccharides that constitute the extracellular material. Researchers detected contaminants such as aluminum, silicon, and iron on the surface of the nodule, which have links to the residual molecules produced during the breakdown of cuticular keratin. [8] In P. hortae , the extracellular cementing material that holds the nodule together is likely the primary factor responsible for protecting the fungus against environmental attack and desiccation. Moreover, this compact organization can also impair successful treatment, which may explain why an untreated black piedra may run a very chronic course.

retrieved_docs.title: Hydroxyapatite Dental Material -- Issues of Concern
retrieved_docs.content: Several methods exist for producing hydroxyapatite from synthetic or natural sources. Synthetic hydroxyapatite is derived from raw materials such as calcium carbonate, calcium hydroxide, calcium nitrate, diammonium hydrogen phosphate, and ammonium hydroxide. The fabrication process of hydroxyapatite involves both wet methods and solid-state reactions, followed by calcination or sintering. These methods utilize chemical reactions that adjust the content of CaO and TCP to achieve the stoichiometric conditions necessary for hydroxyapatite formation.

retrieved_docs.title: Zirconia Biomaterials -- Clinical Significance
retrieved_docs.content: This material is a non-cytotoxic metal oxide, is insoluble in water, and has no potential for bacterial adhesion. In addition, it has radio-opacity properties and exhibits low corrosion. These are inert material and exhibit biocompatibility. Zirconia implants osseointegrate as well as titanium implants.

retrieved_docs.title: Hydroxyapatite Dental Material -- Definition/Introduction
retrieved_docs.content: The dental role of hydroxyapatite is significant, as it constitutes 70% to 80% of the weight of dentin and enamel. Enamel, the hardest substance in the human body, is made up of relatively large hydroxyapatite crystals measuring 25 nm thick, 40 to 120 nm wide, and 160 to 1000 nm long. [6] Unlike bone, enamel lacks collagen; instead, amelogenins and enamelins provide a framework for mineralization. Hydroxyapatite is the primary component of enamel, helping to minimize diffuse reflectivity by filling surface pores, which contributes to the semitranslucent appearance of enamel. [4] [7]

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 5 -- Level 3 -- Community 2015
retrieved_docs.content: Ferric hydroxide and calcium phosphate are both categorized as types of industrial aid, indicating that they are substances used to support and facilitate various industrial processes. As forms of industrial aid, ferric hydroxide and calcium phosphate serve functional roles such as acting as additives, precipitants, or raw materials that enhance manufacturing, treatment, or production operations. Their classification under the broader concept of industrial aid underscores their importance in improving efficiency and outcomes in industrial settings.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 3 -- Community 2002
retrieved_docs.content: Industrial Aid encompasses chemical substances such as ferric hydroxide and calcium phosphate. Ferric hydroxide is classified under the broader category of Industrial Aid and is also linked to the Czech term “železité sloučeniny,” indicating its origin as an iron compound. Calcium phosphate similarly falls within the Industrial Aid category, highlighting its role as a material used to support various industrial processes. Recognizing ferric hydroxide and calcium phosphate as components of Industrial Aid helps clarify their applications in manufacturing, water treatment, and other industrial contexts.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 11 -- Level 3 -- Community 1716
retrieved_docs.content: of biogenic calcium carbonate materials. Collectively, these relationships illustrate the versatility of calcium carbonate across pharmaceutical categories: it functions as a filler, a calcium source, a buffering or antacid agent, and a structural constituent in both synthetic and natural products, highlighting its importance in formulation science and patient care.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 3 -- Community 1986
retrieved_docs.content: Selenium is recognized as a mineral and, at the same time, belongs to the broader category of těžké kovy (heavy metals). As a trace element within the minerály (minerals) group, selenium’s classification under both minerals and heavy metals highlights its dual chemical identity and informs its relevance in health, toxicology, and environmental contexts.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 3 -- Level 3 -- Community 1910
retrieved_docs.content: Ferric hydroxide is identified as being derived from the class of substances referred to as železité sloučeniny, which translates to “iron compounds.” This relationship indicates that ferric hydroxide belongs to the broader category of iron‑based chemical entities, reflecting its composition as an iron(III) hydroxide formed from the transformation or processing of iron compounds.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 10 -- Level 3 -- Community 1296
retrieved_docs.content: Chlorbutanol and calcium phosphate are both classified as industrial aids, meaning each serves as a chemical agent used to facilitate various industrial processes. As types of industrial aid, these substances are employed to improve efficiency, act as catalysts, or modify material properties in manufacturing contexts, highlighting their functional role in industry rather than being primary end‑products.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 7 -- Level 3 -- Community 1877
retrieved_docs.content: Selenium is categorized as a mineral, indicating that it belongs to the broader group of minerály and is recognized as an essential trace element within this class.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 9 -- Level 2 -- Community 727
retrieved_docs.content: IRON/MULTIVAMINS LIQUID and Multivitamin tablet both contain vitamin D as an ingredient, indicating that vitamin D is a common component of these supplement formulations. A specific form of vitamin D referred to as inhibitory kostní resorpce functions to inhibit bone resorption, highlighting its role in preserving bone integrity. Vitamin D is also related to هشاشة العظام (osteoporosis), underscoring the importance of adequate vitamin D intake for the prevention and management of bone‑weakening conditions.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 20 -- Level 3 -- Community 2462
retrieved_docs.content: Bulk compounding chemicals represent a category of pharmaceutical excipients that includes several inorganic substances. Within this group, železo (iron) is recognized as a related component, and the category also comprises calcium oxide and calcium phosphate dibasic as specific bulk compounding agents. Additionally, chelátory železa (iron chelators) are identified as derivatives that have iron (železo) as their parent compound, linking the chelating agents directly to the iron element within the broader context of bulk compounding chemicals. This interconnected framework highlights how iron, calcium-based compounds, and their respective chelators are organized under the umbrella of bulk compounding chemicals, informing formulation strategies and material selection in pharmaceutical manufacturing.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 3 -- Level 1 -- Community 126
retrieved_docs.content: connects ferric maltol, ferric hydroxide and sodium ironedetate; ferric maltol is derived from the source compound pyrony, while ferric hydroxide originates from the broader category of iron compounds (železité sloučeniny). This class represents oral agents that deliver trivalent iron for the treatment of anemia. Industrial Aid is a higher‑level category that includes ferric hydroxide and calcium phosphate as subordinate types, indicating their use in industrial applications such as corrosion control, water treatment or as raw materials. MISCELLANEOUS DRUG CATEGORIES is positioned under both the replacement‑preparation hierarchy and the broader urikosurika grouping, serving as an umbrella for drug classes that do not fit into more specific categories. Mercurial diuretic is identified as a low‑ceiling diuretic (excluding thiazides) within the “TYPES AND MISCELLANEOUS DIURETICS” parent category and is associated with organokovové sloučeniny (organometallic compounds), which in turn

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 15 -- Level 2 -- Community 706
retrieved_docs.content: Bulk compounding chemicals represent a broad category of pharmaceutical and inorganic substances that include isopropamide iodide, iron (železo), calcium oxide, and calcium phosphate dibasic. Each of these individual agents is considered a member of the bulk compounding chemicals group, and the group itself is classified as a type of tricalcium phosphate. Within this hierarchy, calcium oxide is further identified as an oxide, as indicated by its mapping to oxidy. Iron (železo) serves as the parent compound for iron chelators (chelátory železa), linking chelation agents directly to their iron source. This integrated framework highlights how diverse chemical entities—ranging from organic iodide compounds to essential minerals and their derivatives—are organized under a common bulk compounding chemicals classification, facilitating a clearer understanding of their relationships for formulation, therapeutic use, and pharmacological management.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 23 -- Level 3 -- Community 2203
retrieved_docs.content: to calcium carbonate, consistent with the mineral composition of nacreous shells. Collectively, these relationships illustrate calcium carbonate’s multifaceted presence in veterinary and human medicines—as an inert filler, a therapeutic calcium source, a contributor to electrolyte homeostasis, and a material foundation for biologically derived substances.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 2 -- Level 3 -- Community 1459
retrieved_docs.content: Calcium phosphate functions as an industrial aid, meaning it is employed in manufacturing and processing contexts to support or improve industrial operations. The concept of industrial aid therefore includes substances such as calcium phosphate that assist in various industrial processes.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 21 -- Level 1 -- Community 148
retrieved_docs.content: activity, phosphate chelation, inhibition of bone resorption) and their synergistic inclusion with iron, calcium, other vitamins and trace minerals underpin their therapeutic, preventive and formulation‑stabilizing roles in patient care.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 19 -- Level 3 -- Community 2290
retrieved_docs.content: Bulk compounding chemicals are a class of substances used in pharmaceutical preparation, and within this class calcium oxide, calcium phosphate dibasic, and tricalcium phosphate are identified as specific members. Calcium compounds represent another category of calcium‑based substances, comprising calcium acetate, calcium oxide, calcium phosphate dibasic, and tricalcium phosphate. Consequently, calcium oxide, calcium phosphate dibasic, and tricalcium phosphate are recognized both as bulk compounding chemicals and as calcium compounds, highlighting their dual role in formulation processes and calcium chemistry. This overlap underscores the importance of these agents in both compounding practices and calcium‑related therapeutic applications.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 32 -- Level 1 -- Community 200
retrieved_docs.content: acid with calcium are specifically categorized within the vitamin C combinations group, reflecting their dual role as antioxidant and mineral supplements. This integrated classification clarifies the relationships among bulk‑forming laxatives, calcium‑related agents, diagnostic radioisotopes, and vitamin C–based products, highlighting their relevance to patient care, clinical investigation, and therapeutic use.
